channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Some US businesses close in a 'day without immigrants.' But many say they can't lose income
item.description: Several businesses from day cares to grocery stores and hair salons closed across the United States in a loosely organized day of protest against the President Donald Trump's immigration policies.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto And Melissa Perez Winder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: County passes resolution calling for 'dignified treatment' along border
item.description: UPDATE: El Paso County Commissioners Court unanimously passed a resolution on Monday, Feb. 3 on immigration, calling for "dignified treatment of all." EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- El Paso County Commissioners Court is scheduled to discuss and possibly approve a resolution at its meeting on Monday, Feb.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors who supported Menendez brothers' release accuse DA of retaliation
item.description: Erik and Lyle have spent nearly 35 years in prison for the brutal killing of their parents, Jose and Kitty Menendez, with a shotgun inside their Beverly Hills home on Aug. 20, 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Musk adds Shell, Nestlé, Lego to X advertiser 'boycott' lawsuit
item.description: X is adding several more companies to its lawsuit accusing firms of conspiring on an advertising "boycott" on the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Salvage crews recover engine, large portion of jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Salvage crews have recovered an engine and large pieces of fuselage and are working to retrieve a wing from the wreckage of a commercial airliner involved in last week's midair collision near Washington’s Reagan National Airport, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk tightens grip on federal government as Democrats raise alarms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk is rapidly consolidating control over large swaths of the federal government with President Donald Trump ’s blessing, sidelining career officials, gaining access to sensitive databases and dismantling a leading source of humanitarian assistance.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Duffy tells DOT to prioritize areas with high birth rates
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy in a memo told the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prioritize communities that have higher birth and marriage rates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms fracking CEO Chris Wright to lead Energy Department
item.description: The Senate on Monday confirmed Chris Wright, the CEO of fracking company Liberty Energy, to lead the Energy Department. The vote was 59-38. Democrat Sens. Michael Bennet (Colo.), Ruben Gallego (Ariz.), Maggie Hassan (N.H.), Martin Heinrich (N.M.), John Hickenlooper (Colo.), Ben Ray Luján (N.M.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Transportation secretary says 'expansive' impact zone was left by Philadelphia medical plane crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The impact area where a medical transportation plane crashed in Philadelphia was notably “expansive,” U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said Monday after viewing the site with investigators and city officials. Duffy said he was struck by “how expansive it was.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge orders acquittal of ex-prosecutor on 1 of 2 counts in misconduct trial over Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — A judge on Monday ordered the acquittal of a former Georgia prosecutor on one of the two misconduct charges against her, ruling that prosecutors at her trial failed to present any evidence the she obstructed a police investigation of the 2020 killing of Ahmaud .Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Starbucks introduces Valentine's Day drinks, new refreshers
item.description: Instead of stuffed teddy bears or roses, Starbucks is gifting its customers new menu offerings this Valentine’s Day. On Monday, the coffee chain announced that it will be adding Blackberry Sage Refreshers to menus for a limited time.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Salvage crews remove large portion of commercial jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: Salvage crews have removed a large portion of a commercial jet from the Potomac River near Washington’s Reagan National Airport on Monday, five days after a midair collision last week that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What is known about the deadly collision between a passenger jet and Army helicopter
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 and an Army helicopter collided in midair near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all 67 aboard in the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Thune on Trump tariffs: 'We’ll see how long' they last
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) told reporters Monday he’s taking a wait-and-see approach to President Trump’s order to place 25-percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico and a 10-percent tariff on China, which sent stock markets dropping.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Recall for broccoli sold at Walmart in 20 states upgraded to highest risk level: FDA
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has upgraded its classification of a broccoli recall initially reported in late December, labeling it as “Class I” — the agency’s most serious recall category.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rep. Crow 'deeply concerned' that Space Force Base being used for ICE operations
item.description: A USNORTHCOM spokesperson told NewsNation affiliate KDVR that ICE's facility requirements include a temporary operations center.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: What is USAID? Explaining the US foreign aid agency and why Trump and Musk want to end it
item.description: In its first two weeks, President Donald Trump’s administration has made significant changes to the U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Experts explain that and more about FEMA
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal disaster aid is nearly everywhere. About 94% of Americans live in a county that has gotten Federal Emergency Management Agency help for disasters since 2011.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Death of teacher with 20 stab wounds to be reexamined as parents settle with city
item.description: The city of Philadelphia settled Monday with the parents of Ellen Greenberg, and the city's medical examiner’s office will take a new look at Greenberg's manner of death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariffs 'not what American economy needs right now': Klobuchar
item.description: “I’ve favored these targeted tariffs, but he is not using a chisel,” Klobuchar added. “He is using a sledgehammer.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Secretary of Defense Hegseth to visit Fort Bliss, southern border
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Fort Bliss and the southern border on Monday, Feb. 3, Fort Bliss announced. Hegseth will visit Joint Task Force North, which is headquartered at Fort Bliss. This organization is part of U.S. Northern Command.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis to pay woman who says Chauvin knelt on her back. Here's how much
item.description: The city of Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to a woman who alleged that former policeman Derek Chauvin pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just months before he used the same maneuver on George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Fort Bliss sergeant helps lead Army's fight against cancer
item.description: “The Army represents the opportunity to double or triple the amount of people on the (national bone marrow registry) each year,” Sutton said.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Baltazar, NewsNation

item.title: Stocks sink as Trump tariffs rattle Wall Street
item.description: The stock market opened with steep losses Monday in the wake of President Donald Trump's aggressive new tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese goods.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico after they pledge to boost border enforcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is holding off on his tariff threats against Mexico and Canada for 30 days after the two U.S. neighbors agreed to boost border security efforts.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Capitol Police recruit killed in Georgia car crash, other injured recruits airlifted to hospital
item.description: Democratic congressional leadership said U.S. Capitol Police Recruit Officer Christopher Katz died in a fatal car crash in Georgia that injured other recruits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man hit in head by debris from Philadelphia plane crash that broke through diner window
item.description: A Philadelphia diner patron was injured Friday when a piece of debris from a medevac jet that crashed hurtled through a window and hit the man in the head.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Philadelphia plane crash investigators recover medical jet's black box
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board has recovered two importance pieces from the wreckage of the medevac plane that crashed in Philadelphia last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu heads to US to discuss 'victory over Hamas' with Trump
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Sunday he will discuss "victory over Hamas,” countering Iran and expanding diplomatic relations with Arab countries in his meeting with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Passengers, crew killed in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia identified
item.description: Authorities said a seventh person, who had been in a car at the time of the crash, has died. Their identity has not yet been released.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's second week in office delivers jolts and chaotic orders with a mix of politics and tragedy
item.description: President Donald Trump's second week in office seemed to deliver a daily dose of deliberate jolts for the country. There were chaotic reminders of his first term. The White House found itself backtracking on its directive to freeze federal spending on grants and loans.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump puts tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China, spurring trade war as North American allies respond
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Saturday signed an order to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada and China, drawing swift retaliation and an undeniable sense of betrayal from the country's North American neighbors as a trade war erupted among the longtime allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ZEKE MILLER, ROB GILLIES and CHRISTOPHER SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Robert Santos decides to resign as US Census Bureau director midway through a 5-year term
item.description: Robert Santos, who emphasized inclusivity and outreach to overlooked communities, has decided to resign as director of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4
